reasoning: The Lean hypotheses `hp : Nat.Prime p` and `hp1 : Odd p` correctly encode "odd prime". The sum `∑ i ∈ Finset.range (p-1), 1/(i+1)` expands to 1/1 + 1/2 + ... + 1/(p-1), matching the informal harmonic sum exactly. The conclusion `∀ a b : ℤ, (a/b : ℚ) = S → p ∣ a` is equivalent to the informal "if S = a/b then p | a": writing S = c/d in lowest terms, any integer representation is (a,b) = (kc, kd) with k ≠ 0 (the b = 0 case is vacuous since S > 0), so "p | a for all such a" is equivalent to "p | c", which is the standard meaning of the informal claim regardless of whether one reads "a,b" as lowest terms or an arbitrary representation. No hypotheses are silently added or dropped.
